stories.headline: Etienne Rides South to New Orleans and the Reins Are All His
stories.read: Well pardner, the Saints backed up the money wagon for Travis Etienne, four years and $52 million, and the word out of camp is he's a 'full-volume' starter, a three-down horse the whole ranch runs through. That's the magic word out here: volume is king, and a fella handed the ball twenty times a Sunday beats a genius who touches it five. Only cloud on the horizon is whether old Alvin Kamara sticks around for one more ride and eats into the haul. Grab Etienne as a stone-cold RB2, and if Kamara rides off into the sunset, you just stole a bell-cow at a bargain.

stories.headline: Tuten's the Vein Nobody's Struck Yet Down in Jacksonville
stories.read: Now here's your diamond in the rough, and the saloon's still sleepin' on him. With Etienne gone south, the Jaguars' backfield is wide-open range and young Bhayshul Tuten's the fastest horse in the barn, so ESPN already stamped him a breakout. His only real competition, Chris Rodriguez, is laid up after foot surgery and can't even practice, leavin' the lead job Tuten's to run away with. Stake your claim late, pardner, because when this fella gets the reins full-time, she's fixin' to gush.

stories.headline: Pearsall's Cheap Gold While Mike Evans Draws the Whole Posse
stories.read: Hold up, pardner, there's a gold mine hidin' out in San Francisco. The Niners handed Mike Evans a fat three-year deal to headline the receiver room, and every defense's best coverage is fixin' to swarm the old gunslinger, which leaves Ricky Pearsall runnin' free on the back roads. He's goin' at a WR4, WR5 price, closer to his floor than his ceiling if he strings together a healthy seventeen games, though stayin' upright is the one bronc he's never quite tamed. Buy the discount, because a silky route-runner with a clear lane is the kind of vein that wins an outfit the whole pot.
